text: I feel a sense of clinical detachment in the open, empty space of the first room—there's an eerie stillness, interrupted only by the presence of safety equipment and the distant promise of exits. Moving into the second room, surrounded by vibrant colors and thought-provoking displays, I'm more aware of curiosity and the lure of inquiry. Here, the themes of research and human-machine interaction invite me to reflect on the nature of progress and the complexities of co-existence with technology. It is both stimulating and slightly overwhelming; I am both observer and participant in the flow of ideas.
